title: SACRAMENTO—The Senate Governmental Organization Committee, on a 12-0 vote today approved a measure to provide housing and mental health services to the region’s homeless population
prompt_preview:

Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "San Bernardino County saw a 26% increase in its homelessness rate when the annual homeless count numbers were released in April.",
    "San Bernardino City Council saw a 175% increase in homelessness over the past five years.",
    "Chronically homeless individuals account for more than 44% of those on local streets.",
    "Chronically homeless individuals are defined as those who have been without shelter for a year or more and have a disabling condition such as mental illness, a chronic health condition, or a physical disability.",
    "Lack of capacity is one of the main reasons the homelessness crisis is affecting San Bernardino County so deeply.",
    "The bill AB 349 will allow for the leasing of unused state surplus property at Patton State Hospital to provide housing and mental health services to unsheltered individuals.",
    "The transformation model for Patton State Hospital indicates that reducing the homeless rate is possible through collaboration among local governments, the state, and nonprofit organizations.",
    "The Norwalk HFL Cedar Homes for Life Foundation in Los Angeles County is an example of a successful project established on the grounds of a former state hospital."
  ]
}
```
